$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 1960s (telegram / trackside wire-report voice), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically via the 'headlines' PCG32 stream (a pure function of masterSeed + round). Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references into 'pools' — so a handful of templates and pools yield thousands of distinct dispatches. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}. Add new eras/events by adding JSON only — no code. 1960s copy is uppercase-leaning wire-report prose, STOP-punctuated where a telegram would break.

1960s: FROM THE CIRCUIT, WIRE REPORT.
TRACKSIDE DISPATCH — {race}, {year}.
BY TELEGRAPH FROM THE PADDOCK.
OUR CORRESPONDENT CABLES FROM {race}.
WIRE FILED AT THE FALL OF THE FLAG.
The garage celebrates long into the evening. STOP.
Rivals will note the name; the season is far from run. STOP.
A drive the crowd will not soon forget.
The record book keeps the rest.
More of the same, and the paddock will talk of little else.
The mechanics wheel the car away in silence. STOP.
There will be quieter words back at the works.
A weekend the team will wish to forget.
Better days lie ahead, the paddock trusts.
The stopwatch tells no kind story this day.
default: The team regroups for the next round.

1960s: In the championship {player} sits {champPosition}, {champMove}.
The title table now reads {player} {champPosition} — {champMove} on the season.
{player} stands {champPosition} in the running for the crown, {champMove}.
against a car reckoned good only for {expected}
having been fancied no better than {expected}
from a machine the timekeepers pegged at {expected}
{pool:dateline} {player} took the flag first at {race}, a commanding win for {team}. Leading home a field of {fieldSize}, the drive brooked no argument. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} Victory at {race}. {player} controlled the {year} affair from the front, the {team} machine never headed once clear. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} WINS {race}. STOP. The chequered flag falls to {team} and to a driver in imperious form, {expected} on the form book be damned. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} won {race} for {team}, leading a field of {fieldSize}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}

1960s: {pool:dateline} A rostrum finish for {player}, home {position} at {race}. {team} split the front-runners in a field of {fieldSize}, {winner} taking the honours. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} carried {team} to {position} at {race}, champagne on the podium and a drive {pool:expectationBeat}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} PODIUM FOR {player}. STOP. {position} at {race}, behind only {winner} at the flag. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} brought the {team} car home {position} at {race}, points in the ledger from a field of {fieldSize}. {winner} won the day. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} A useful afternoon: {player} scored at {race}, {position} at the flag. No fireworks, but the championship account ticks over. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} GIANT-KILLING AT {race}. STOP. {player} dragged the {team} machine to {position} {pool:expectationBeat}, and the paddock took note. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} {player} made the {team} car sing at {race}, {position} from a field of {fieldSize} and the drive of the day by common consent. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} An off-song afternoon for {player}, {position} at {race} when {expected} was the reasonable hope. {team} will seek answers. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} {player} short of pace at {race}, home only {position} in a field of {fieldSize}. {winner} led a race that never came to the {team} driver. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} was below par at {race}, only {position} when {expected} was expected. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeBad}

1960s: {pool:dateline} HEARTBREAK FOR {player}. STOP. The {team} machine expired at {race}, no fault of the driver, from what had promised {expected}. {winner} took the win. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} Smoke and silence at {race}: mechanical failure sidelined {player} while running its race. The car cried enough. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} {player} OFF THE ROAD AT {race}. STOP. A moment's misjudgement ended the run and left {team} to count the cost. {winner} led home a field of {fieldSize}. {pool:closeBad}
{pool:dateline} A costly slip: {player} threw away {race} by his own hand, out where {expected} had beckoned. {pool:closeBad}
default: {player} crashed out of {race} through driver error. {pool:closeBad}

1960s: {pool:dateline} A workmanlike run for {player}, {position} at {race} in a field of {fieldSize}. Little to report; the miles were logged and the car came home. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
{pool:dateline} No headlines for {player} at {race}, home {position} behind {winner}. A quiet afternoon for {team}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
default: {player} ran to {position} at {race}, a quiet afternoon for {team}. {pool:champLine} {pool:closeGood}
